title: गोपाल स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गोपाल स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
मन्दारमूले मदनाभिरामं बिम्बाधरापूरितवेणुनादम्।
गोगोपगोपीजनमध्यसंस्थं गोपालमीशं परिभावयामि॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥

गोपालं गोपवेषाढ्यं गोपगोपीजनप्रियम्।
गोवर्धनधरं कृष्णं वन्दे गोकुलनायकम्॥१॥

नीलमेघनिभं श्यामं पीताम्बरधरं प्रभुम्।
श्रीवत्साङ्कितवक्षस्कं कौस्तुभप्रभया युतम्॥२॥

मुरलीधरमाद्यन्तं बर्हापीडविभूषितम्।
कुण्डलद्वयशोभाढ्यं वनमालिनमीश्वरम्॥३॥

नन्दनन्दनमानन्दं यशोदाहृदयप्रियम्।
व्रजानन्दकरं बालं वृन्दावनविहारिणम्॥४॥

नवनीतप्रियं बालं गोपीलोचनसुन्दरम्।
दधिमन्थनचोरं तं नमामि मधुसूदनम्॥५॥

कालिन्दीकूलसंचारं राधामाधवसुन्दरम्।
रासमण्डलमध्यस्थं नमामि वृषभानुजम्॥६॥

गोवर्धनगिरिं हस्ते सप्ताहं धारयत्प्रभुम्।
इन्द्रगर्वविनाशाय नमामि कमलापतिम्॥७॥

कालियनागमर्दनं गोपबालसहायकम्।
यमुनापुलिने नृत्यं कुर्वन्तं नमनं मम॥८॥

पूतनाप्राणहर्तारं शकटासुरभञ्जनम्।
तृणावर्तान्तकं बालं वन्दे गोपालसुन्दरम्॥९॥

वत्सपालं चारुहासं माखनस्तेयकारिणम्।
दामबन्धनलीलाढ्यं वन्दे दामोदरं प्रभुम्॥१०॥

गोचारणं वनोद्यानं वेणुवादनतत्परम्।
सखिभिः सह खेलन्तं वन्दे गोपकुलोद्भवम्॥११॥

सर्वलोकमहेशानं सर्वदेवनमस्कृतम्।
गोपवेषधरं विष्णुं वन्दे गोपालनन्दनम्॥१२॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
गोपालस्तोत्रमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेद्भक्तिसंयुतः।
गोपालकृष्णसायुज्यं प्राप्नोति परमं पदम्॥

सन्तानसुखमाप्नोति धनधान्यसमृद्धिकम्।
गोसम्पत्तिं महालक्ष्मीं लभते नात्र संशयः॥

॥ इति श्री गोपाल स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Gopala Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Mandara-mule Madana-abhiramam Bimba-adhara-apurita-venu-nadam.
Go-gopa-gopi-jana-madhya-samstham Gopalam-isham Paribhavayami.

|| Stotram ||

Gopalam Gopa-vesha-adhyam Gopa-gopi-jana-priyam.
Govardhana-dharam Krishnam Vande Gokula-nayakam.||1||

Nila-megha-nibham Shyamam Pitambara-dharam Prabhum.
Shrivatsa-ankita-vakshaskam Kaustubha-prabhaya Yutam.||2||

Murali-dharam-adyantam Barha-pida-vibhushitam.
Kundala-dvaya-shobha-adhyam Vana-malinam-ishvaram.||3||

Nanda-nandanam-anandam Yashoda-hridaya-priyam.
Vraja-ananda-karam Balam Vrindavana-viharinam.||4||

Navanita-priyam Balam Gopi-lochana-sundaram.
Dadhi-manthana-choram Tam Namami Madhusudanam.||5||

Kalindi-kula-sancharam Radha-Madhava-sundaram.
Rasa-mandala-madhyastham Namami Vrishabhanujam.||6||

Govardhana-girim Haste Saptaham Dharayat-prabhum.
Indra-garva-vinashaya Namami Kamala-patim.||7||

Kaliya-naga-mardanam Gopa-bala-sahayakam.
Yamuna-puline Nrityam Kurvantam Namanam Mama.||8||

Putana-prana-hartaram Shakata-asura-bhanjanam.
Trinavarta-antakam Balam Vande Gopala-sundaram.||9||

Vatsa-palam Charu-hasam Makhana-steya-karinam.
Dama-bandhana-lila-adhyam Vande Damodaram Prabhum.||10||

Go-charanam Vanodyanam Venu-vadana-tatparam.
Sakhibhih Saha Khelantam Vande Gopa-kulodbhavam.||11||

Sarva-loka-maheshanam Sarva-deva-namaskritam.
Gopa-vesha-dharam Vishnum Vande Gopala-nandanam.||12||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Gopala-stotram-idam Punyam Yah Pathed-bhakti-samyutah.
Gopala-Krishna-sayujyam Prapnoti Paramam Padam.

Santana-sukham-apnoti Dhana-dhanya-samriddhikam.
Go-sampattim Mahalakshmim Labhate Natra Samshayah.

|| Iti Shri Gopala Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान — मन्दार वृक्ष के नीचे, कामदेव के समान सुन्दर, बिम्बफल जैसे अधरों पर वेणु बजाते, गो-गोप-गोपियों के मध्य विराजमान गोपाल ईश्वर का मैं भावपूर्वक ध्यान करता हूँ।

गोपवेष धारी, गोप-गोपी जनप्रिय, गोवर्धनधारी कृष्ण, गोकुलनायक को मैं वन्दन करता हूँ। नीलमेघ समान श्यामवर्ण, पीताम्बरधारी, श्रीवत्स अंकित वक्ष, कौस्तुभ से युक्त।

नन्दनन्दन, आनन्दस्वरूप, यशोदा के हृदयप्रिय, व्रज में आनन्द करने वाले, वृन्दावन में विहार करने वाले बालकृष्ण। नवनीत (मक्खन) प्रिय, दधिमन्थन चोर, माखनचोर, कालिन्दी तट पर विचरने वाले, रासमण्डल में विराजमान, राधामाधव।

गोवर्धन पर्वत को सात दिन हाथ पर उठाने वाले, इन्द्र के गर्व को नष्ट करने वाले, कालियनाग का मर्दन करने वाले, पूतना-शकटासुर-तृणावर्त को मारने वाले बालगोपाल।

इस स्तोत्र के भक्तिपूर्वक पाठ से गोपालकृष्ण का सायुज्य, सन्तान सुख, धन-धान्य समृद्धि और महालक्ष्मी प्राप्त होती है।
benefits: Invokes the Bala-Gopala (child Krishna) form for innocence and divine play
Covers all childhood lilas of Krishna from birth to Vrindavana
Invokes progeny, child welfare, and protection of children
Invokes the blessing of wealth in cattle (go-sampatti) and agricultural abundance
Deepens devotion through remembrance of Krishna's sweet pastimes
Leads to sayujya (union) with Gopala Krishna and supreme liberation
description: Dedicated to Lord Krishna, the Gopala Stotram (गोपाल स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. This sacred composition is sourced from Traditional and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant progeny, child welfare, and protection of children as well as lead to sayujya (union) with Gopala Krishna and supreme liberation. Additionally, it is known to cover all childhood lilas of Krishna from birth to Vrindavana. This sacred text is ideally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Janmashtami and Gopashtami for enhanced blessings. Comprising 12 verses, it can be completed in approximately 8 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Gopala Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Gopala Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer to child Krishna (Bala Gopala) for devotion, progeny, and divine grace.

seoFaqs.question: Which scriptural source does the Gopala Stotram come from?
seoFaqs.answer: The most circulated Gopala Stotram is the Gopala Sahasranama Stotram, a 1000-name hymn extracted from the Brahma Vaivarta Purana, where it is narrated by Lord Shiva to Goddess Parvati on Mount Kailasa. The text contains 193 verses in classical Sanskrit anushtubh metre that string together the 1000 names. Shorter Gopala stotras, including the Gopala Ashtakam and Bal Gopala Stotram, are independent compositions extracted from devotional poetry of the Pushti Marg and Gaudiya Sampradaya traditions, but the Sahasranama recension preserved in Brahma Vaivarta Purana is the canonical reference work for the term 'Gopala Stotram' in pan-Indian Vaishnava liturgy.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Gopala-Krishna and how does this differ from Dwarka-Krishna or Bhagavad Gita-Krishna?
seoFaqs.answer: Gopala (literally 'cow-protector') refers specifically to the Vrindavan-Vraja phase of Krishna's lila when he lived as the foster-son of Nanda and Yashoda, grazing cows in the forests of Gokula, Vrindavan, Nandagram and Barsana. This phase is described primarily in the tenth canto of the Bhagavata Purana, in the Harivamsha (a Mahabharata supplement), and in the Brahma Vaivarta Purana. Dwarka-Krishna by contrast is the adult king who established the Yadava capital at Dwarka after leaving Mathura, and Gita-Krishna is Arjuna's charioteer at Kurukshetra. Gaudiya, Pushti Marg and Nimbarka traditions all hold the Gopala-aspect as the most rasamaya (sweetness-saturated) of Krishna's manifestations.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Gopala Sahasranama specifically promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti at the conclusion of the Gopala Sahasranama explicitly promises four categories of benefit: (1) santati-prapti (the granting of children, especially male progeny, which is why the stotram is the principal childlessness-remedy in Vaishnava tradition); (2) shatru-vinasha (destruction of enemies and obstacles); (3) lakshmi-prapti (acquisition of wealth and household prosperity); and (4) ultimately Krishna-sayujya (oneness with Krishna in Goloka Vrindavana). Shiva tells Parvati that even one careful recitation of the 1000 names secures freedom from rebirth in lower wombs. Couples performing a 40-day or 108-day anushthana for progeny are advised one recitation per day at Brahma Muhurta in front of a Bal Gopal idol.

seoFaqs.question: When is the Gopala Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gopala Stotram is recited on Janmashtami (Krishna's birthday in Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami), on Nanda Utsav (the day after Janmashtami), on Radhashtami (Bhadrapada Shukla Ashtami), throughout the entire Karthika month (the most sacred month for Krishna devotion), on Govardhan Puja, on every Ekadashi, and on every Shukravara (Friday) sacred to Lakshmi-Krishna. Couples praying for progeny are advised daily Brahma Muhurta recitation through the entire eighth lunar month from a sankalpa Janmashtami. ISKCON, Gaudiya Math, and Pushti Marg temples include extracts from the Gopala Sahasranama in their daily Mangala-Aarti immediately after the Brahma-Samhita.

seoFaqs.question: Are there ritual rules for chanting the Gopala Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional manuals prescribe a simple panchopachara puja before recitation: a clean asana (preferably wool or kusha grass), a Bal Gopal or Krishna idol or framed image with tulsi mala, a ghee lamp, fresh tulsi leaves (the most beloved offering to Krishna), white sandal paste, fresh flowers (preferably yellow chameli or champa), and a small naivedya of butter, mishri (rock sugar), or makhan-mishri-the foods most associated with the cowherd Krishna's childhood. Onion, garlic, and meat are strictly avoided in the household for the duration of any anushthana. The recitation should ideally be in front of a tulsi plant if possible, and concluded with the offering of arghya (water) and chanting of 'Hare Krishna Maha-mantra' three times.

seoFaqs.question: How does Gopala Sahasranama compare with Vishnu Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are 1000-name stotras of the Vishnu deity-stream, but they differ in source, mood, and use. The Vishnu Sahasranama is from the Anushasana Parva of the Mahabharata, taught by Bhishma to Yudhishthira on the bed of arrows, and addresses the cosmic, dharmic, all-pervading aspect of Vishnu (Narayana). The Gopala Sahasranama is from the Brahma Vaivarta Purana, taught by Shiva to Parvati on Kailasa, and addresses the intimate, lila-saturated, sweetness-aspect of Krishna in his Gopala (cowherd-boy) form. The Vishnu Sahasranama is typically recited at funerals, on Saturdays for Saturn-relief, and as a meditation on cosmic dharma; the Gopala Sahasranama is typically recited for progeny, household sweetness, and Vrindavan-bhakti.
